title: [InstCombine] Support folding intrinsics into phis
summary:
The developers generalized a pattern where an intrinsic call (like `llvm.is.fpclass`) with a PHI operand can be folded into the PHI's incoming values, similar to existing select folding. The key insight was recognizing that speculatable intrinsics without cross-lane operations can be safely moved across control flow into predecessor blocks.

The patch extends the existing `FoldOpIntoSelect` logic to also handle PHI nodes by adding a check for `PHINode` operands and calling `foldOpIntoPhi`. This allows the intrinsic to be evaluated on each incoming value separately, potentially enabling further simplifications (e.g., constant folding). The `dropUBImplyingAttrsAndMetadata` call ensures safety when speculating instructions.

This approach transforms a case-specific optimization into a reusable framework applicable to many intrinsics (e.g., `llvm.fabs`, `llvm.umax`, floating-point min/max), as shown in the generalized test suite. Developers can apply this by identifying speculatable operations with PHI/select operands and leveraging the existing folding infrastructure.
